architecture.summary: HSAE jointly trains a series of Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) at different granularity levels, learning parent-child relationships between features across levels through a structural constraint loss and random feature perturbation mechanism. Information flows from LLM activations into hierarchically organized SAEs that decompose features into monosemantic atoms and compose them into semantically meaningful tree structures, recovering multi-scale conceptual hierarchies.
trained_components: hierarchical sparse autoencoders (SAEs at multiple levels)

biblio.title: From Atoms to Trees: Building a Structured Feature Forest with Hierarchical Sparse Autoencoders

claims.statement: HSAE consistently recovers semantically meaningful hierarchies across various LLMs and layers
HSAE preserves the reconstruction fidelity and interpretability of standard SAEs across different dictionary sizes
Structural constraint loss and random feature perturbation mechanism strengthen the alignment between parent and child features
Feature splitting phenomenon indicates that LLMs capture hierarchical structure in natural language
HSAE provides a scalable tool for discovering and analyzing multi-scale conceptual structures embedded in LLM representations
